payload.source_text: The pocket money tracker system allows children to claim pocket money tasks as they are completed throughout the week.
Parents can detail tasks that should be completed and the monetary amount earned for that task.
As a task is completed it is linked to the child that completed it.
The children should be able to see the available tasks and the tasks they have completed that week.
At the end of each week the children can see the total amount they have earned that week.
The Parents can also see a report of how much each child has earned and the tasks they completed so that they can provide the pocket money earned to each child.
At the start of the next week all tasks become available again to be claimed.
Historical task data shall be available.
The resulting system shall be able to operate on iOS 16, Android 9 and Windows 11 or later.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL ZachmanCell elements present in the ledger.

payload.description: Claiming completed tasks
payload.justification: Claiming is a core strategic process performed by children throughout the week to obtain credit for completed tasks.

payload.description: Specifying task requirements and rewards
payload.justification: Parents perform this strategic process to define what tasks are available and what they are worth.

payload.description: Distributing pocket money
payload.justification: Distribution is a strategic process that parents perform based on reporting visibility, representing the culmination of the pocket money cycle.

payload.description: Maintaining historical records
payload.justification: Record maintenance is a strategic process for continuity and accountability purposes.

payload.description: Providing task visibility to children
payload.justification: Visibility provision is a strategic function serving the motivational need for children to see available and completed tasks.

payload.description: Providing weekly earnings totals
payload.justification: Providing totals is a strategic function enabling awareness and accountability for children.

payload.description: Providing reporting visibility to parents
payload.justification: Reporting visibility is a strategic function enabling parents to accurately distribute pocket money.

payload.description: Pocket money task with monetary reward
payload.justification: Tasks are the central thing of strategic importance - they are claimed by children, specified by parents, and have visibility requirements. Multiple signals reference this entity. | Rewards are explicitly associated with tasks and represent a distinct thing of strategic importance in the pocket money domain.

payload.description: Weekly earnings total
payload.justification: Weekly earnings totals are a distinct strategic entity provided to children for awareness and accountability.

payload.description: Historical record
payload.justification: Historical records are maintained as a distinct thing of strategic importance for continuity and accountability purposes.

payload.description: Task completion status
payload.justification: Completion status is a key attribute distinguishing available from completed tasks, critical to the claiming and visibility requirements.

payload.description: Task requirement specification
payload.justification: Requirements are attributes of tasks that parents need to specify, representing what must be done to earn the reward.

payload.description: Task assigned to child
payload.justification: The relationship between tasks and children is strategic - children claim tasks and need visibility into available versus completed tasks.

payload.description: Reward associated with task
payload.justification: The association between tasks and monetary rewards is explicitly stated as a parental specification requirement.

payload.description: Weekly pocket money cycle
payload.justification: The weekly cycle is strategically important - children claim tasks throughout the week and receive weekly earnings totals.

payload.description: Task completion claim
payload.justification: The claiming of a completed task is a strategically significant event triggering credit and potential payment.

payload.description: Child
payload.justification: Children are key strategic actors who claim tasks, view task visibility, and receive earnings totals.

payload.description: Parent
payload.justification: Parents are key strategic actors who specify tasks and rewards, and require reporting visibility for distribution.

payload.description: Enable children to claim credit for completed tasks
payload.justification: This is explicitly stated as the core purpose - a primary strategic goal of the enterprise.

payload.description: Motivate children through task visibility and accountability
payload.justification: Motivational need for visibility into available and completed tasks represents a strategic goal driving enterprise behavior. | Awareness and accountability are explicitly stated purposes of providing weekly earnings totals, representing strategic goals.

payload.description: Enable accurate pocket money distribution
payload.justification: Accurate distribution is the explicitly stated purpose driving the need for parental reporting visibility.

payload.description: Maintain continuity and accountability
payload.justification: Continuity and accountability are stated as the purposes for maintaining historical records, representing strategic goals.

payload.description: Parents must specify task requirements before assignment
payload.justification: The parental need to specify requirements and rewards implies a strategic constraint on how tasks enter the system.

payload.description: Children claiming completed pocket money tasks
payload.justification: Signal SG922 defines the core business process of children claiming completed tasks.

payload.description: Parent defines tasks with monetary values
payload.justification: Signal SG923 establishes parent capability to define task entities with associated monetary values as a business function.

payload.description: Child views available tasks
payload.justification: Signal SG925 defines child actor visibility requirement for available task entities as a business function. | Signal SG925 defines child actor visibility requirement for completed task entities as a business function.

payload.description: Child accesses weekly earnings summary
payload.justification: Signal SG926 specifies child actor access to aggregated earnings summary as a business function.

payload.description: Parent accesses child earnings reports for payment reconciliation
payload.justification: Signal SG927 defines parent actor access to earnings reports for reconciliation as a business function.

payload.description: Weekly task availability reset cycle applies
payload.justification: Signal SG928 establishes the business rule governing weekly task availability reset.

payload.description: Historical task data must be retained and accessible beyond current cycle
payload.justification: Signal SG929 mandates retention and accessibility as a business rule governing historical data.

payload.description: Task with associated monetary value
payload.justification: Signal SG923 explicitly identifies task entities with monetary value attributes defined by parents.

payload.description: Child entity associated with task completion
payload.justification: Signal SG924 establishes child as a distinct entity in relationship with completed tasks.

payload.description: Completed task entity
payload.justification: Signals SG922 and SG924 both reference completed tasks as distinct entity state relevant to claiming and relationships.

payload.description: Child earnings report aggregating weekly task completion data
payload.justification: Signal SG926 identifies a distinct aggregated data object representing child earnings at period end. | Signal SG927 describes a distinct reporting entity for parent access containing aggregated task completion data.

payload.description: Historical task data
payload.justification: Signal SG929 establishes historical task data as a distinct retained entity beyond current cycle.

payload.description: Completed task linked to child
payload.justification: Signal SG924 explicitly specifies the conceptual relationship linking completed task entities to child entities.

payload.description: Weekly pocket money claiming cycle
payload.justification: Signal SG922 defines a weekly cycle as the temporal context for pocket money task claiming.

payload.description: Weekly task availability and visibility reset cycle
payload.justification: Signal SG925 establishes a weekly timeframe as the temporal constraint for task visibility. | Signal SG928 explicitly establishes a weekly reset cycle governing task availability.

payload.description: Weekly period end for earnings summary access
payload.justification: Signal SG926 identifies period end as a distinct temporal event triggering earnings summary availability.

payload.description: Child claiming tasks and viewing earnings
payload.justification: Signals SG922, SG925, and SG926 collectively establish child as a distinct business actor with task claiming and viewing capabilities.

payload.description: Parent defining tasks and accessing earnings reports
payload.justification: Signals SG923 and SG927 collectively establish parent as a distinct business actor with task definition and reporting access capabilities.

payload.description: Enable payment reconciliation for parents
payload.justification: Signal SG927 identifies payment reconciliation as the business objective for parent access to earnings reports.

payload.description: Historical task data must remain accessible beyond current cycle
payload.justification: Signal SG929 mandates retention and accessibility as a business constraint governing data lifecycle.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL CellContentItem elements present in the ledger.

payload.name: Family Participation Structure
payload.description: Defines who participates in the household accountability system and their fundamental roles within the family enterprise relationship.

payload.name: Task Accountability Framework
payload.description: Establishes the enterprise commitment to transparent task assignment, completion verification, and reward fulfillment within the household.

payload.name: Financial Obligation Fulfillment
payload.description: Governs the enterprise responsibility for accurate calculation and timely distribution of earned compensation to household members.

payload.name: Record Retention Stewardship
payload.description: Ensures the enterprise maintains authoritative historical records to support continuity, auditability, and long-term accountability.

payload.name: Parental Oversight Transparency
payload.description: Provides enterprise commitment to visibility mechanisms enabling parental governance and monitoring responsibilities.

payload.name: Task Entitlement Governance
payload.description: Business responsibility for establishing and governing the catalog of compensated tasks, including their monetary obligations and availability cycles within household accountability structures.

payload.name: Task Completion Accountability
payload.description: Business responsibility for recording and attributing completed task obligations to participants, establishing the basis for earnings accountability and persistent record of fulfillment.

payload.name: Earnings Record Stewardship
payload.description: Business responsibility for maintaining authoritative records of participant earnings derived from task completion, ensuring persistent accessibility for reconciliation and accountability across cycles.

payload.name: Payment Settlement Reconciliation
payload.description: Business responsibility for enabling verification and settlement of monetary obligations arising from completed tasks, supporting parent accountability for discharge of payment duties.

payload.name: Task Participation Entitlement
payload.description: Business responsibility for enabling child participants to view and claim available task opportunities within established governance cycles and availability rules.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Domain elements present in the ledger.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall recognise children as participants in the family accountability system.
payload.rationale: Establishes the structural participation relationship between children and the enterprise accountability system at the scope level.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall recognise parents as participants in the family accountability system.
payload.rationale: Establishes the structural participation relationship between parents and the enterprise accountability system at the scope level.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall establish a task accountability framework that assigns tasks to children and associates rewards with those tasks.
payload.rationale: Expresses the structural commitment to a task-child-reward relationship at enterprise scope, derived from the entity (pocket money task), assignment relationship (task to child), and reward association relationship.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall maintain task requirement specifications for all assigned tasks.
payload.rationale: Combines the attribute (task requirement specification) with the process of specifying requirements into a single enterprise obligation to maintain clear task definitions.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall maintain task completion status for all assigned tasks.
payload.rationale: Derives from the task completion status attribute; enterprise accountability for tracking completion state.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall provide visibility of available tasks to children.
payload.rationale: Combines the function of providing task visibility with the motivation to enable transparency and accountability, expressed as an enterprise commitment.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable children to claim credit for completed tasks.
payload.rationale: Unifies the claiming process, task completion claim event, and the goal of enabling children to claim credit into a single enterprise capability commitment.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall specify monetary rewards for tasks.
payload.rationale: Expresses the enterprise commitment to define reward amounts as part of task specification, derived from the process of specifying rewards.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall maintain weekly earnings totals for household members.
payload.rationale: The What-column Entity 'Weekly earnings total' requires the enterprise to structurally maintain this financial record as part of its accountability framework.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall provide weekly earnings totals to household members.
payload.rationale: The How-column Function 'Providing weekly earnings totals' expresses an enterprise capability obligation to deliver compensation information.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall distribute earned compensation to household members.
payload.rationale: The How-column Process 'Distributing pocket money' establishes the enterprise obligation to execute the distribution function.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall operate on a weekly compensation cycle.
payload.rationale: The When-column Cycle 'Weekly pocket money cycle' constrains the temporal governance of the compensation obligation.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall ensure accurate distribution of earned compensation.
payload.rationale: The Why-column Goal 'Enable accurate pocket money distribution' expresses a governance commitment to accuracy in fulfilling financial obligations.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall maintain historical records to support continuity and long-term accountability.
payload.rationale: The Why-column CCI establishes the governance obligation (continuity and accountability), while the What and How CCIs specify the object (historical records) and the enterprise commitment (maintaining). This is a constraint because it expresses an enterprise-level stewardship obligation grounded in the Why column's motivational content.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall provide parents with visibility into task reporting.
payload.rationale: Establishes the enterprise commitment to transparency mechanisms that enable parental oversight of assigned tasks.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall ensure that parents specify task requirements prior to task assignment.
payload.rationale: Governance constraint requiring parental definition of task parameters as a prerequisite to assignment, enforcing parental control over delegated responsibilities.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain a record of each task with its associated monetary value.
payload.rationale: Business responsibility to maintain the catalog of compensated tasks. WHAT-column entity (Task) combined with HOW-column capability (Parent defines tasks with monetary values) yields a capability statement. CCI-ROW2-C-What-001 names the entity; object-recursion produces a structural obligation for that entity below.

payload.statement: A task shall consist of an associated monetary value.
payload.rationale: Object-recursion from the Task entity in the functional requirement above. The WHAT-column CCI explicitly names the composition: 'Task with associated monetary value'. This is a structural assertion about the entity.

payload.statement: Parent shall be accountable for defining tasks and accessing earnings reports.
payload.rationale: WHO-column CCI names an accountable business role (Parent) with two defined responsibilities. The two-step 'and' test: (1) is there a single responsibility that subsumes both? Yes — the Parent's role-level accountability for task governance and reporting access. Therefore, a single statement is appropriate rather than splitting.

payload.statement: The business shall enforce a weekly reset cycle for task availability.
payload.rationale: HOW-column Rule ('Weekly task availability reset cycle applies') combined with WHEN-column Cycle yields a governance constraint the business must enforce. The weekly cycle is a normative restriction on business behaviour.
payload.fit_criteria: Task availability state resets on a seven-day cycle boundary.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain a record of each completed task.
payload.rationale: Derived from the completed task entity; the business must maintain records of task completion as a persistent business responsibility.

payload.statement: A completed task shall be attributed to a specific child participant.
payload.rationale: Expresses the structural relationship between completed task and child, establishing the accountability linkage.

payload.statement: The business shall be able to record a child's claim of a completed pocket money task.
payload.rationale: Derived from the claiming process and the child actor role; expresses the business capability to accept and record task claims.

payload.statement: The child participant shall be able to view their accumulated earnings.
payload.rationale: Derived from the child actor's viewing capability; the business must make earnings information available to the child participant.

payload.statement: The business shall govern task claiming on a weekly cycle.
payload.rationale: Derived from the weekly claiming cycle; expresses the temporal governance rule constraining when claiming occurs.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain a record of each participant's earnings derived from completed tasks.
payload.rationale: Business capability to record participant earnings tied to task completion, enabling the weekly earnings summary access described in How-004.

payload.statement: The business shall make available to each child participant a weekly earnings summary aggregating completed task data.
payload.rationale: Business obligation to provide child access to their own earnings summary at weekly period end, supporting transparency and participant accountability.

payload.statement: The parent shall be able to access child earnings reports for payment reconciliation.
payload.rationale: Business capability enabling parent role to fulfil payment reconciliation responsibility against completed task earnings.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain historical task data accessible beyond the current cycle.
payload.rationale: Business rule governing the persistent accessibility of historical task records, ensuring accountability and reconciliation across cycles regardless of cycle state.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain a child earnings report for each participant.
payload.rationale: WHAT-column entity at business level requires the business to maintain the artefact; Object-recursion surfaces the structural composition in REQ-002.

payload.statement: A child earnings report shall aggregate weekly task completion data.
payload.rationale: Object-recursion from REQ-001; the entity 'child earnings report' carries an explicit composition assertion (aggregates weekly task completion data).

payload.statement: The business shall make child earnings reports available to the parent for payment reconciliation.
payload.rationale: HOW-column business capability (parent accesses reports) combined with WHO-column actor (parent) and WHY-column motivation (enable payment reconciliation); single unifying capability subsumes all three CCIs.

payload.statement: The business shall govern earnings summary access on a weekly period-end basis.
payload.rationale: WHEN-column event expressing a temporal governance rule on when access is available; framed as a business constraint rather than a technical scheduling function.

payload.statement: The business shall enable child participants to claim completed tasks for which they are entitled to compensation.
payload.rationale: Derived from the business capability to support children claiming pocket money tasks and the actor responsibility for task claiming. Expresses the business obligation to enable the claiming capability.

payload.statement: The business shall make available to child participants the set of tasks they are entitled to view.
payload.rationale: Derived from the function enabling children to view available tasks and the actor viewing earnings. Expresses the business responsibility to maintain task visibility for entitled participants.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain a weekly cycle governing task availability and visibility for participants.
payload.rationale: Derived from the temporal cycle CCI describing weekly reset of task availability. Expresses the business obligation to govern task lifecycle on a defined cadence.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Requirement elements present in the ledger.

payload.description: Section: High Level Description

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Segment elements present in the ledger.

payload.description: Establishes the core purpose of enabling children to claim completed pocket money tasks throughout the week.
Defines the parental motivation to specify task requirements and associated monetary rewards.
Expresses the motivational need for children to have visibility into available and completed tasks.
Articulates the purpose of providing weekly earnings totals to children for awareness and accountability.
Defines the parental need for reporting visibility to enable accurate pocket money distribution.
Indicates the purpose of maintaining historical records for continuity or accountability.
Section header indicating the beginning of conceptual system overview content.
Defines the core business concept of children claiming completed pocket money tasks on a weekly cycle.
Establishes parent actor capability to define task entities with associated monetary value attributes.
Specifies the conceptual relationship linking completed task entities to child entities.
Defines child actor visibility requirements for available and completed task entities within weekly timeframe.
Specifies child actor access to weekly aggregated earnings summary at period end.
Defines parent actor access to child earnings reports aggregating task completion data for payment reconciliation.
Establishes the business rule for weekly task availability reset cycle.
Mandates retention and accessibility of historical task entity data beyond current cycle.
